name: The Stepwise Informativeness Assumption: Why are Entropy Dynamics and Reasoning Correlated in LLMs?
authors: Mar Gonzàlez I Català (first author) + 3 co-authors [uncertain]
dataset_size: N/A — framework/theory paper, not a benchmark
domain: N/A — framework/theory paper (empirical validation on math/commonsense reasoning)
white_box_requirements: Per-token predictive distribution (logits) over generation; conditional answer entropy H(answer | reasoning prefix) must be computable, which requires either teacher-forcing the candidate answer through the model or marginalizing over a small answer set. Gray-box logit access to a single forward pass is sufficient.
supervision: unsupervised (theoretical / analytic framework — no labels required at test time)
granularity: per-step over reasoning prefix; aggregated trajectory-level signal of answer entropy descent
temporal_resolution: streaming / per-step (conditional answer entropy is defined at every reasoning prefix length n) — the framework analyses the trajectory H(answer | x_{1:n}) across n
integration_mode: post-hoc / analytic framework — provides a theoretical justification for entropy-trajectory hallucination detectors rather than a new detector itself [uncertain]
spectral_signature_overlap: low — the paper analyses entropy dynamics in the time domain (descent profiles, characteristic signatures) and does NOT use eigenvalues, Dirichlet energy, spectral entropy, or any frequency-domain features. It provides a theoretical lens that justifies why time-domain entropy trajectories correlate with correctness, but does not pre-empt frequency-domain spectral fusion of H(n).
tested_models: Gemma-2, LLaMA-3.2, Qwen-2.5, DeepSeek and Olmo variants (open-weight families, range of sizes)
handles_long_context: false [uncertain] — focus is on reasoning chains (GSM8K/ARC/SVAMP), not long-document RAG
tested_at_what_length: reasoning trace lengths typical of GSM8K/ARC/SVAMP (hundreds of tokens) [uncertain]
reported_metric: correlation between conditional answer entropy descent and correctness; characteristic-signature classification accuracy [uncertain]
reported_value: [uncertain] — paper is primarily theoretical/analytic; no single headline AUC is reported in available abstracts
overlapping_benchmark: GSM8K (we use it; paper uses it for empirical SIA validation)
model_used_in_paper: Gemma-2, LLaMA-3.2, Qwen-2.5, DeepSeek, Olmo (multiple sizes) [uncertain on exact sizes]
applicability: high — this paper explicitly NAMES our hypothesis (entropy-spike-as-failure-signal becomes a corollary of SIA) and provides the theoretical scaffolding we have been missing. It strengthens our framing without competing with our spectral-fusion contribution.
preemption_risk: MEDIUM as theoretical pre-emption, LOW as empirical pre-emption. The paper formalises 'conditional answer entropy as a progress variable' and predicts that 'characteristic signatures of [entropy] descent indicate whether reasoning converges reliably'. This is exactly our working hypothesis, now named and grounded in maximum-likelihood theory. HOWEVER: (a) they analyse the time-domain descent profile, not spectral / Fourier / Nadler-fused features of H(n); (b) they target reasoning correctness on math/commonsense (GSM8K, ARC, SVAMP) and do not address agentic or RAG long-form generation; (c) they do not propose a detector — they provide the JUSTIFICATION for why detectors that read entropy trajectories work. Our spectral / Nadler-fusion contribution remains novel; we should now CITE this paper as the theoretical basis and reposition our work as 'spectral instantiation of SIA-induced characteristic signatures'. Our scope claim (works on reasoning, not factual QA) is also independently validated by their reasoning-only empirical scope.
bracha_alignment: moderate — SIA frames entropy as an information-theoretic progress variable with predictable descent. This is a natural target for COIN / LTT calibration: e.g., test-time conformal bounds on the SIA-implied entropy descent rate could yield distribution-free risk control over reasoning-correctness predictions. Bracha's PPI machinery could also calibrate SIA-based detectors against weak labels.
ofir_alignment: moderate-to-high — SIA's 'characteristic signatures of entropy descent' is a one-dimensional projection of a richer trajectory; multi-view kernel and spectral methods (VSDE / PRAE / STG) can extract the higher-order signatures (variance, periodicity, Dirichlet energy on the trajectory graph) that SIA predicts but does not extract. This is exactly Ofir's framing: SIA gives us the why, multi-view spectral fusion gives us the how.
notes: CRITICAL FOR THESIS FRAMING. This is the closest theoretical paper to our hypothesis to date. Recommended actions: (1) cite as theoretical basis in next thesis draft; (2) reposition Phase-9 negative result (factual QA fails) as consistent with SIA's reasoning-only scope; (3) verify whether the paper's 'characteristic signatures' overlap with any of our FEAT_NAMES — if they only use mean/slope, our spectral features (sw_var_peak, STFT peaks) are an orthogonal contribution; if they propose richer descriptors, we need to differentiate carefully. arXiv ID 2604.06192 is consistent with a March-2026 submission. Cannot independently verify all author names; full author list flagged uncertain.
public_code_url: [uncertain] — no code repository surfaced in search; paper appears primarily theoretical
model_size_range: open-weight families spanning ~1B–70B [uncertain on exact range]
gpu_requirements: negligible — conditional answer entropy is a single-forward-pass metric; replication scale matches model inference cost on GSM8K/ARC/SVAMP (single A100 sufficient for ≤13B models) [uncertain]
sample_size_for_replication: [uncertain] — paper covers full GSM8K/ARC/SVAMP test sets
min_samples_for_replication: ~200–500 reasoning traces for stable correlation estimates [uncertain]
runtime_estimate: single forward pass per reasoning trace + answer-conditional re-scoring; comparable to standard semantic-entropy probe (~seconds per sample on A100) [uncertain]
handles_step_localization: true — conditional answer entropy is defined per prefix length n, so step-level localisation is in principle supported [uncertain]
cumulative_state_modeling: Trajectory of H(answer | x_{1:n}) treated as a progress variable; 'characteristic signatures of descent' are functionals over the full trajectory (not just last step). Closest existing analogue is Φmin / monotonicity-violation framing.
confidence_masking_addressed: Partially — the SIA framework is REASONING-specific by construction (it requires answer-informative prefixes, which only exist when the trace carries reasoning content). This implicitly explains why factual short-form QA (low SIA) fails for entropy-trajectory detectors, aligning with the Cheng et al. 2025 confidence-masking observation, but the paper does not reference that work directly [uncertain].
